title: Define misclassification classification taxonomy and evidence contract
description:
## Summary
Define the operator-facing taxonomy and evidence contract for per-item misclassification classification in evaluations.

## Deliverables
- Lock the four required categories: `score_configuration_problem`, `information_gap`, `guideline_gap_requires_sme`, and `mechanical_malfunction`.
- Define what evidence is available at item scope and what cannot be inferred at item scope.
- Define the classifier output contract for one item, including primary category, rationale, confidence, and evidence snippets.
- Explicitly keep feedback contradictions / label inconsistency analysis out of this classifier.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Kickoff: starting taxonomy/evidence-contract work for misclassification classification so downstream pipeline, persistence, and dashboard tasks can share one stable schema. Immediate step: define category set, required evidence fields, and deterministic output contract boundaries for item-level + evaluation-level signals.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Implemented misclassification taxonomy/evidence contract in shared RCA utilities and wired it into persisted RCA payloads.

Shipped:
- Locked category set: score_configuration_problem, information_gap, guideline_gap_requires_sme, mechanical_malfunction.
- Explicit excluded analyses in contract: feedback_label_contradictions and label_inconsistency_audit.
- Item-scope evidence availability vs non-inferable-at-item-scope boundaries.
- Item classifier output contract (primary_category, rationale, confidence, evidence_snippets schema).
- RCA payloads now include misclassification_classification_contract in both standard and small-set RCA flows.

Validation:
- pytest -q plexus/rca_analysis_test.py plexus/Evaluation_feedback_test.py
- 18 passed.
